title: You’re injecting the jaw wrong. Male vs female Injection points.
filename: You’re injecting the jaw wrong. Male vs female Injection points..en.vtt
excerpt: On this week's show we're discussing male and female jaw lines the differences and the best place to inject a male's jaw I'm going to be sharing my top tips for injecting men's Jewel we're going to be talking a little bit about the ratio between cheeks and jaws and I'm going to s…

segments.text: on this week's show we're discussing male and female jaw lines the differences and the best place to inject a male's jaw I'm going to be sharing my top tips for injecting men's Jewel we're going to be talking a little bit about the ratio between cheeks and jaws and I'm going to show you some cases where it's gone a bit wrong so that you don't make the same mistake so recently Zac Efron got a lot of attention that he probably didn't want because his face appeared to change now he says he hasn't had any plastic surgery or injectables but he does look a lot like someone who might have had such an experience I don't know for sure but what I see that's changed on his face is still useful for us to learn from what's obvious to me is that the proportions are different he has a wider chin he has a wider jawline and his lips are Fuller and this makes his top and bottom part of his face no longer match in quite the way that they're used to and he certainly shifted away from a kind of boyish charm into a very masculine but then slightly odd proportioned male face so we can learn a lot about out firstly

what you can do to a jawline to mask analyze but also how you can go a little bit too far and create something that doesn't quite fit the existing structures remember all of beauty is essentially self-referential so the image that you see is only beautiful when it refers appropriately to the other parts of the face and when you change too much in one place you can break those ratios and make it look essentially unnatural which our brains are very Adept at picking up that's essentially what I think has happened with Zac Efron so let's discuss now what male beauty is at least from my perspective I think it's very different to female Beauty now of course what I'm talking about here is masculine men who want to look masculine and other people may want to feminize their face and that's a different topic for a different show if a man is pursuing masculine Beauty we need to understand what that is as opposed to feminine beauty now the biggest difference I think actually lies around the ratio between the cheeks and the chin and this is one of the thing things I see quite often popping up on

social media is kind of an obsession on figuring out how to inject male lips or male cheeks and actually the biggest thing that you do differently with male lips and male Cheeks is you inject them a lot less because male beauty is actually more about jaw lines dominating over cheeks if you want to upgrade a female's appearance often what you do is make the chin look more petite and the cheeks look more dominant it's exactly the opposite with a male we make the jawline more dominant and the cheeks look smaller this is essentially what differentiates male and female faces the most in my view we also have other elements like the brow Ridge and the size of the nose narrow ellipse but the thing that we can inject is without doubt the jawline so what are men pursuing it's very important if you're Consulting a man who wants to look masculine that you choose your words appropriately essentially most men are not that Keen on being described is becoming more beautiful they want to be

handsome or rugged or athletic so choose your words appropriately but they also then guide your treatment strategy because if a man wants to look masculine which often means as athletic or strong it's about enhancing angles and proportions we want to give the impression of a strong jaw large bones and strong muscles and I actually personally think that if you can see some of the anatomy underneath it does not degrade a man in quite the same way as it might a woman so this essentially might indicate kind of lines or Shadows that indicate having a low volume of fat can sometimes be associated with being athletic and therefore also good looking in a way that with women is less true I think the ideal with woman is more around volume health and good volume rather than an athletic appearance of course there's room for both in our in terms of what we find beautiful but it's more that way with men so let's look back at Zac Efron's face and try and understand a bit more about what could have gone

wrong here now one thing's for sure is he hasn't been feminized his chin is clearly squarer and you can see it appears like he has less volume in his cheek because of this contrast between cheek and jawline and also his lips have changed in my view this is one of the things that's most striking is actually the fact that his teeth are less visible in some of the images that you see because his lips are larger and I do wonder if his lips had not been treated and his jaw had been treated whether you would have had quite the same response because I think the lips are playing a huge role I also think his type the type of beauty that he has the type of looks he has is more soft and rounded and actually quite an adolescent kind of small featured type of masculine Beauty he looks like a good looking teenage boy in his prime and now he's become a kind of masculine but slightly odd looking version of himself and it could simply be the contrast between those two types of beauty that's not quite right remember whenever you treat your patients try and figure out I think

most people have an underlying philosophical type of beauty is it a kind of youthful baby face beauty or a statuesque type of beauty and when you understand that you can augment or alter or improve in the way that is consistent with the existing structure so let's consider the other side of the spectrum some men will end up looking more feminine after a treatment and I think this is quite often because still ninety percent of all treatments are on women and this is because there's a difference in how men and women value appearance at different age I think generally I think women are more interested in Beauty than men as a as an average and I don't think that's ever going to change actually although people constantly say it is changing all that happens is that more women get interested in our treatments in the similar rate than men so it's always about 90 10 so about 90 female 10 male and so the average clinician is much more used to treating female faces and we tend to ER towards more female treatments the classic example would be when treating forehead with botch lime

toxin a lot of men end up with eyebrow lifts because we worry about dropping females eyebrows and female eyebrows tend to be arched and then if you look back at say for example the case of Wayne Rooney he ended up with some rather strange arched eyebrows and that's because that was a feminine pattern of treatment delivered on a male face he actually would have been fine with a brow drop because that's his Natural State in many ways is a heavy brow the same thing happens with cheeks men booking for a full face restoration to look younger and they end up erring more in a female style treatment so that means additional volume in the cheeks they may have volume in their jaw as well but the cheek volume is a very feminizing thing a large voluminous soft cheek is not masculine and so the more you put in order to create you know fewer lines and shadows and create a bit of definition the more you eventually err on the side of looking feminine and you'll see this even amongst top injectors who are male in our industry will often overdo their cheeks and look a little bit too voluminous from my in

my opinion I might be wrong but that's how I experience these things I think you can spot a man who's been over treated because they have a slightly feminine Vibe about a soft round cheek in a 45 year old man it's not common so let's talk about how to inject male jaw first of all of course we have to be grounded in what male beauty is and it's really about masculinization which is about angles and proportions if you have a look at a good looking man that is usually a ratio which makes the jawline about the same width as the cheek or potentially even more masculine would be that the jawline is dominating the cheek mostly we're about the same or slightly narrow about 10 degrees difference between the cheek and the jawline so let's get down to how to actually inject the jawline which needs to be grounded in what we're actually aiming for now the male jawline is different to female jaw lines because it is wider relative to the cheeks and there is an angle which is stronger typically now if you look at a natural untreated female

jawline that someone is beautiful you tend to have a curve to the jawline there's a gentle curve it can be nearly straight but there's nearly always a gentle curve whereas it can be considered more masculine to have a straighter jawline here so we may be aiming for a straighter line between the GoNoodle angle and the angle of the chin that's the next part to consider as well is that line although it's straight from certain angles can also curve outwards so it's more acceptable in a man to add additional volume here to make it straight and make the jawline slightly curve outwards than it would be in a female next is the angle on the chin and this is where men are most different there tends to be a straighter line here where the chin meets the jawline so you have a point here and a point here as opposed to what's common on more female faces is that there is a low Point here where the chin curves down almost like a single point where there is a the sharpest change in angle so this is the one of the core differences with how we inject because every angle that I've discussed is a

place where it can be reasonable to inject to create more of an angle so the most common injection points to masculize a face would be on this angle here if you drew a line past the oral commissure and you created an angle here or enhanced an angle here and did the same on the other side you'd be masculizing the lower face this is particularly true because that point is more lateral than even on a woman with a squarer chin it should be parallel with the nostrils on a female case rather than parallel with the oral commissures jawline can also be lower as well so this is very important Point very easy to make this mistake female faces heart-shaped male faces squarer and this involves the GoNoodle angle being lower so on a male you may inject the GoNoodle angle and you can actually even lower the gonor angle without making them look female in fact you'd be making them look more masculine whereas on a female if you're injecting the GoNoodle angle and you lower it even slightly you effectively make them look like they have a bigger jawline even if

it looks straight and petite from one angle it's the lowness of this point relative to the cheek that makes the patient look more masculine so you can do this on purpose with a man we can inject the good New Angle but inferior to it and actually make it slightly lower where appropriate the same point you can also make it wider and this from the frontal view is the most masculizing thing you can do is to create a wideness to the lower face which you can do with cannula and with needle but I like to do the bulk of the volumizing on the GoNoodle angle using a needle because this is the way that it stays stable when you move I'm not a big fan of doing it all in a subcutaneous fat where the patient opens their mouth and the filler stays still and you see the jawline emerge from underneath kind of looks a bit odd if you do some of that work on the periosteum with the needle it'll be stable when they move the last tip is to always remember the face in three dimensions the chin in particular is particularly tricky because it's the meeting point of multiple different planes so what injectors quite often find the hardest

is to correct the chin keeping all those planes appropriate in their size and proportion for example consider making a chin longer it's quite common to see chins become pointier and a pointier chin that's longer looks kind of alien-like you see this on male and female faces but if you're trying to masculize a chin it is true that you need to make it longer but not at the expense of also making it wider so it needs to become longer and wider and then you've got the same issue looking from a profile perspective which is are you building out the chin so that it is a continuation of the same surfaces or does it feel like it's kind of jutting out at a new angle that wouldn't fit with natural proportions so when you're injecting you need to be continuously moving your head and looking at the chin in context with all the other planes of the face and elongating it extending it and projecting it all at once in the right proportions not just looking at one angle one position and then getting that correct because you'll find when you look at your before and afters that

you'll spot angles where it just doesn't look right so I hope this video has helped you don't forget to like And subscribe leave us a comment on the video I do read all the comments and also if you would like to see more about jaw lines I have one specifically on the female jawline which you can watch here [Music]
